DISCOVERY 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

DISCOVERY 302, AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES; RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - GRANTED IN PART, OVERRULED IN PART. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) SANCTIONS GRANTED AGAINST DEFENDANT WALGREEN AND ITS ATTORNEYS OF RECORD, BURNHAM BROWN, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1594 MADE PAYABLE TO THE CLIENT TRUST FUND OF CALLOWAY & WOLF. SANCTIONS TO BE PAID NO LATER THAN JULY 13, 2020. SANCTIONS ARE STAYED AND ARE TO BE LIFTED BY THE TRIAL COURT AT A LATER DATE. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 3.132 AND APPROVED AS TO FORM BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM KATHERINE GALLO AT KLGALLO@DISCOVERYREFEREE.COM. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-08-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-06-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BAY VALLEY SECURITY, L.L.C., MERVYN OGHOGHO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' Bay Valley Security and Mervyn Oghogho's motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied.

On a motion for summary judgment, the moving party "bears the burden of persuasion that there is no triable issue of material fact and that he is entitled to judgment as a matter of law" and the "burden of production to make a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any triable issue of material fact." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) Defendants fail to meet their burden.

First, Defendants fail to show that there is no duty. "It also is . . . established that, as a general matter, there is no duty to act to protect others from the conduct of third parties . . . But . . . courts have recognized exceptions to the general no-duty-to-protect rule" such as the "special relationship doctrine." (Delgado v. Trax Bar & Grill (2005) 36 Cal.4th 224, 235.) One area in which courts have found such a special relationship is that between security guard and store patron, as presented by the facts here. (Marois v. Royal Investigation & Patrol, Inc. (1984) 162 Cal.App.3d 193, 199-200 ["[A] security guard hired by the business should be liable to an injured customer where the guard fails to act as would a reasonable security guard under similar circumstances and that failure causes the customer's injury. By contracting with the business to provide security services, the security guard creates a special relationship between himself and the business's customers. This relationship, in and of itself, is sufficient to impose on the guard the obligation to act affirmatively to protect such customers while they are on the business premises."].)

Second, Defendants fail to show that there is lack of a triable issue fact as to whether Mr. Oghogho's actions were the proximate cause of Plaintiff's injuries. Defendants present no evidence in support of their argument. The only facts they provide are that "the guard was, like, grabbing this shoplifter and they were tugging and tussling, the shoplifter broke free of the security guard's holding - grip. And . . . the shoplifter barreled right into me." (TOE, Ex. C, 126:15-127:2.) This evidence does not "fail[] to show that [the] breach contributed to plaintiff's injuries." (Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 774-76.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Amended Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Interrogatories; Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Production Of Documents; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
R. Boone Callaway, Esq. Callaway & Wolf, 415-541-0300, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
Erica Summan, Esq., Burnham Brown, 510-835-6756, appearing for Defendant Walgreens Co.;(CourtCall)
Kristina M. Pedroso, Wood Smith Henning & Berman, 925-222-3400, appearing for Defendant Bay Valley Security LLC; (CourtCall)
No other appearance.

All parties present stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Katherine Gallo hearing today's motion.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

PLAINTIFF STACY KNIGHT Amended Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Interrogatories; Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Production Of Documents; And For Monetary Sanctions

Form Interrogatories12.4 and 15.1: Granted. All objections are overruled. Verified responses to be served no later than July 13, 2020.

Special Interrogatory #1: All objections are overruled. Denied as it is in conflict with C.C.P. 2025.230.
Special Interrogatory #11: Granted. Objection to the term "fully" is sustained and removed from the interrogatory. All other objections are overruled. Verified responses to be served no later than July 13, 2020.

Requests # 8, 13, 14, 16: There is no ruling as there has been a subsequent response.
Request : All objections are overruled. However, the court finds that the request is overbroad. Defendant to produce the insurance policy only. See Irvington-Moore v. Superior Court (1993) 14 CA4th 733, 737.
Request #11: Granted. All objections are overruled.
Request #12: Granted. All objections are overruled.
Request #13: Parties have stipulated to modifications to request #13.
Request #15: Objection as it is not limited in time is sustained. All other objections are overruled. The request is limited in time as to the time of the incident.
Defendant to serve a verified response in compliance with C.C.P. 2031.210-240 and produce documents to Requests , 11, 12, 13, and 14 no later than July 13, 2020.

In reviewing the discovery responses, the court questions whether defendant met its obligations in making a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information before responding to the discovery. See Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496. A party cannot plead ignorance to information, which can be obtained from sources under his control. Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771, 782 A party cannot plead ignorance to information, which can be obtained from sources under his control. Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771, 782 This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Superior Court (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 161 CA 3d 151, 167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552. See Weil and Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2019) 8:1051-1060. An attorney must act with due diligence before responding to discovery. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390. Therefore, Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted against Defendant Walgreen and its attorneys of record, Burnham Brown, in the amount of $1594 made payable to the Client Trust Fund of Calloway & Wolf. Sanctions to be paid no later than July 13, 2020. Sanctions are stayed and are to be lifted by the Trial Court at a later date. Counsel for defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order pursuant to CRC 3.132 and approved as to form by email to Judge Pro Tem Katherine Gallo at klgallo@discoveryreferee.com. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
